title: Logical error: 'current_writer != nullptr'
description:
### Describe the bug
Nasty setup with 2 disks with different metadata types.
### How to reproduce
Setup minio running with `tests/docker_scripts/setup_minio.sh stateless` then use this config for the server:
```xml
<storage_configuration>
<disks>
<disk0>
<type>object_storage</type>
<object_storage_type>s3</object_storage_type>
<metadata_type>plain</metadata_type>
<endpoint>http://localhost:11111/test/data0</endpoint>
<access_key_id>clickhouse</access_key_id>
<secret_access_key>clickhouse</secret_access_key>
</disk0>
<disk1>
<type>object_storage</type>
<object_storage_type>s3</object_storage_type>
<metadata_type>local</metadata_type>
<endpoint>http://localhost:11111/test/data1</endpoint>
<access_key_id>clickhouse</access_key_id>
<secret_access_key>clickhouse</secret_access_key>
</disk1>
</disks>
<policies>
<policy0>
<volumes>
<volume0>
<disk>disk0</disk>
<disk>disk1</disk>
</volume0>
</volumes>
</policy0>
</policies>
</storage_configuration>
```
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tuple() SETTINGS non_replicated_deduplication_window = 2, storage_policy = 'policy0';
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0) VALUES (1); --Logical error
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
<Fatal> : Logical error: 'current_writer != nullptr'.
<Fatal> : Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):
0. src/Common/StackTrace.cpp:389: StackTrace::StackTrace() @ 0x0000000014463be4
1. src/Common/Exception.cpp:56: DB::abortOnFailedAssertion(String const&) @ 0x00000000143bd395
2. src/Storages/MergeTree/MergeTreeDeduplicationLog.cpp:246: DB::MergeTreeDeduplicationLog::addPart(String const&, DB::MergeTreePartInfo const&) @ 0x000000001eaf6399
3. src/Storages/MergeTree/MergeTreeSink.cpp:239: DB::MergeTreeSink::commitPart(std::shared_ptr<DB::IMergeTreeDataPart>&, String const&) @ 0x000000001ec383a5
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) PRIMARY KEY (id);
CREATE TABLE t2 AS SELECT * FROM t1;
INSERT INTO t2 (id, name) SELECT id, name FROM t1 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Different metadata types for disks in storage configuration
